title: Student Fee Advisory Committee
text: sdonfack@ucsc.edu; SFAC meets weekly during the academic year. Meetings are open to the public, and all students are welcome to attend. Each meeting begins with a public comment period, providing an opportunity for any student to share feedback or ask questions. Join us via Zoom and be part of the conversation.; Spring 2026 Meetings are scheduled on Tuesdays from 3:30 – 5:00 pm between March 31st and June 9th. For meeting location or Zoom information, please contact sfac@ucsc.edu.; Agendas and minutes are posted weekly to keep our community informed.; Agendas and Minutes; Stay Informed: This week’s agenda; Submit a written public comment; Dia de lose muertos ; The Student Fee Advisory Committee (SFAC) is pleased to announce its 2025-2026 funding call.

title: Student Fee Advisory Committee
text: Members of the campus community are invited to submit proposals for Student Services Fee and Equity in Mental Health funds. The call provides an opportunity to request support for programs and services that enhance student life, well-being, and success. SFAC will carefully review all proposals and make funding recommendations based on impact, alignment with student needs, and available resources.; Applications were due Monday, January 5, 2026 at 5:00 pm and the funding call application process is not closed. The committee is reviewing applications throughout the Winter Quarter.; Funding Call Resources:; 2025-2026 Funding Call Memorandum; Resources for Awardees; Funding Call Appeal Process; Funding Call Appeal Request Form; Summary of 2024-2025 Funding Awards; SFAC is guided by UC and campus policies that govern the use of student fees. These policies help ensure that student fee funds are used responsibly, transparently, and in alignment with student needs and institutional priorities.

title: Student Fee Advisory Committee
text: To promote accountability, SFAC publishes annual reports that provide insight into how the Student Services Fee and Measure 7 are supporting the student experience at UC Santa Cruz.; Explore the policies and reports below to learn more about the governance and impact of student fees.; Annual Reports:; 2024-2025 Student Services Fee Report (SSF); 2024-2025 Campus Programs Fee Report (Measure 7); Policies, Resources and Archived Reports; Student looking at laptop; The following information outlines the different types of fees that students pay as part of their overall cost of attendance, including mandatory tuition and Student Services Fees as well as campus-based fees voted on and passed by students through referenda measures. Other types of fees include course materials fees, summer fees, and miscellaneous fees.; Cost to Attend & Student Budgets; Course Fees; Fee Refund Schedule; Residency for Tuition Purposes; Summer Session Fees; Tuition & Fees;

title: Student Fee Advisory Committee
text: UC-wide mandatory fee for all students set by The Regents used to support student financial aid and related programs, admissions, registration, administration, libraries, operations and maintenance of physical plant of the campus, the University’s operating budget, and all costs related to instruction and faculty salaries. Nonresident Supplemental Tuition is a UC-wide mandatory fee set by The Regents and charged to undergraduate and graduate students who are not residents of California. Review the current fees for undergraduate and graduate students (through the Registrar’s Office).; Student services fees; UC-wide mandatory fee for all students set by The Regents used to support a variety of non-instructional student service activities such as counseling and mental health services, academic advising, tutorial assistance, cultural and recreation programs, and capital improvements, which provide extracurricular benefits for students.; Campus-based fees;

title: Student Fee Advisory Committee
text: Campus-based mandatory fees (also known as referendum) are initiated and approved by students via a student referendum (through the campus elections process) and later approved by The Regents; fees are a condition of enrollment. The type of fees and fee levels vary between undergraduate and graduate students based on the terms of the referendum, and the revenues must be used in accordance with the referendum language. The fees help to support student programming and activities, student facilities, student transportation, student government, childcare, and student health. Campus fees may also be assessed by the Chancellor for safety related reasons such as seismic upgrades for campus buildings.; Miscellaneous & course materials fees;

title: Student Fee Advisory Committee
text: User fees, service charges, or fines assessed to cover the cost of services and materials, which are not mandatory as a condition of enrollment and are only charged to students and others when they actually use the service (e.g., application fees, duplicate diplomas fee, course material fees, lab use fees, etc.).; Find more information about the Miscellaneous and Course Fee Advisory Committee.;

title: Student Health Outreach & Promotion (SHOP)
text: SHOP | Student Health Outreach & Promotion; Request coaching; Students walking across bridge; Judgment-Free. Health-Focused. Always Here.; SHOP is your go-to spot for health-related information, real conversations, and personal growth. Whether you bump into us around campus (we pop up all over!) or visit our office at Porter College, we provide a safe, judgment-free space to explore your health and well-being. Using a public health approach, we empower individuals and our community to make informed decisions that positively impact overall wellness.; Check out our hours and location or get connected via email. Whether you’re curious about SHOP programs, events, or resources, we’d love to hear from you. ; Contact SHOP; SHOP Services; SHOP offers a wide range of services to support UCSC students in maintaining their health and well-being.

title: Student Health Outreach & Promotion (SHOP)
text: From wellness coaching and interactive workshops to tabling at campus events, we’re here to help you thrive. Our peer education teams share resources and support on harm reduction, safer sex, and overall well-being—making it easier to be informed, stay healthy, and feel connected.; Explore SHOP services; Two students walking toward Porter B; Two students sitting by the Porter Koi pond; Well-Being at UCSC; Wellness is something you control and part of your well-being. Think of wellness as an action you take to feel better on a day-to-day basis that leads to better well-being. External factors, like where you live, can affect your well-being, too. SHOP supports students by promoting skills that you can use to thrive at UCSC and beyond.; Learn about well-being resources at UCSC; Sexual Health;

title: Student Health Outreach & Promotion (SHOP)
text: Our highly trained student peer educators, working alongside professional staff, are the heart of our sexual health efforts at SHOP. The Slug Love team promotes reproductive and sexual health through workshops, campus events, the Condom Co-Op, Birth Control Patrol (contraception support), and information on DIY STI testing.; Learn about sexual health resources; The slug love peer team ; Alcohol, Tobacco, and Other Drugs; At SHOP, we believe you can have a fun, social college experience while staying safe. That means partying responsibly, avoiding binge drinking or dangerous substance combinations, and looking out for yourself and others. We’re here to offer honest, factual, and non-judgmental support.; Learn about alcohol, tobacco, and other drug-related information; Harm Reduction & Recovery; The Cove is an inclusive space for students in recovery from alcohol and/or other drugs and anyone interested in or supporting recovery.

title: Student Health Outreach & Promotion (SHOP)
text: Our diverse group represents a range of ages and sobriety experiences and is supported by UCSC staff, faculty, and community members.; Learn more about harm reduction and recovery with The Cove; Bridge by Hahn Student Services; Add SHOP on Instagram!;

title: Student Honors (Office of the Registrar FAQ)
text: HomeSupport & SuccessHonors & Awards ; Honors Opportunities; GPA Criteria for Honors; Undergraduate Awards; Scholar Highlights; At UC Santa Cruz, honors and awards recognize academic excellence and provide opportunities for students who want to challenge themselves and grow. Many students find that joining honors programs or societies creates supportive communities that enrich their academic journey, while others are motivated by the chance to be formally recognized for their achievements. From college and university honors to competitive scholarships and research awards, these opportunities highlight the dedication, creativity, and leadership of UC Santa Cruz undergraduates.; UC Santa Cruz awards several honors for outstanding academic achievement.; Dean’s Honors; Students will be eligible for quarterly Dean’s Honors if they have earned a minimum of 15 units that quarter, of which at least 10 are graded, with a term grade point average (GPA) equal or higher than that required for University Honors at graduation in their group for the current academic year.

title: Student Honors (Office of the Registrar FAQ)
text: The notation “Dean’s Honors” will appear on the transcript.; Honors in the Major; At graduation, the department sponsoring a student’s major program may confer Honors or Highest Honors in the major. This notation appears on the transcript as well as on the diploma. In general, no more than 15 percent of the graduation class in a major will be awarded Honors at graduation.; To be considered for University Honors at graduation, students must have completed 70 or more units at the University of California and have attained in their group a UC GPA that places them in the rankings. Each year and for each group, the Registrar will calculate the GPA thresholds required for these levels of University Honors, based on the GPAs of recent graduates. The notation University Honors will appear on the diploma and transcript.; Honors Criteria: Grade Point Averages by Term;

title: Student Honors (Office of the Registrar FAQ)
text: The Division of Undergraduate Education maintains a listing of the following awards for UCSC undergraduate students. Campus application deadlines are listed for the scholarships.; Deans’, Chancellor’s, and Steck Awards; Dean’s awards are granted to 50 undergraduate research projects, 10 from each of the five academic divisions. Submissions are typically an outstanding senior thesis, or other outstanding research project, completed during the current academic year. Each winning Deans’ award project will receive a $100 award. The top 3 Deans’ Award projects from each division will also receive a Chancellor’s award of $500. The most outstanding Chancellor’s Award research project will receive the Steck Award of $2,000. The names of all winners are displayed on the Deans’ and Chancellor’s Award website and are also usually announced at college commencement ceremonies.; CAMPUS APPLICATION DEADLINE: Annually in early April;

title: Student Honors (Office of the Registrar FAQ)
text: AWARD AMOUNTS: $100 Dean’s, $500 Chancellor’s, $2,000 Steck Award; Award Guidelines; The Office of Campus Advising Coordination supports some of the scholarships available for UC Santa Cruz undergraduate students. Campus application deadlines are listed below for the following opportunities.; Strauss Scholarship; The Donald A. Strauss Scholarship Foundation allows students to pursue a public service project during their junior or senior year. The scholarship provides $15,000 for expenses of the public service project and for some tuition, fees, books, room and board. The Foundation will award scholarships to as many as 15 full-time students who have:; Demonstrated an interest in public service; Outstanding leadership potential; Developed and can demonstrate effective communication skills; The desire to “make a difference” in local, regional, national or international communities; One or two years remaining until graduation; A GPA in the upper 1/3 of their class;

title: Student Honors (Office of the Registrar FAQ)
text: Submitted an innovative, original, public service proposal; Strauss Foundation Website; Award Amount: $15,000; Truman Scholarship; The Truman Scholarship requires an endorsement from the UC Santa Cruz Faculty Advisor. It is recommended that applicants review the requirements and meet with the institutional advisor, Don Bard, before applying (see contact info below).; University junior, 3.5 GPA; Interest in public service, leadership, and public policy.; Award: $30,000 ($15,000 each for first and last years of grad school). ; Truman Scholarship Website; Koret Scholarship; Active student study and work area.; Koret Slam Event; The Koret Research Scholarship is $2,000 award, disbursed through the Financial Aid Scholarship Office, funded by a generous grant from the Koret Foundation. Each year up to 50 students doing a variety of research projects and experiences are awarded.;

title: Student Honors (Office of the Registrar FAQ)
text: Application opens late Sept (beginning of fall quarter); Application closes late October; The Koret Slam is a part of the Koret Scholarship. The Slam is an event where Koret scholars present their work to the public. Koret scholars are also recognized by name in the UC Santa Cruz commencement program at the end of each spring quarter. We are commitment to undergraduate education and student success. Undergraduate participation in research plays a significant role in academic achievement and retention. The Koret Scholarship is just one example of the outstanding research opportunities available to UCSC students. For more information about getting involved in undergraduate research please visit the Undergraduate Research Opportunities website.;

title: Student Services
text: Spend a little time at any of the 10 colleges, and you might forget that UCSC is a large research university. That focus on small-scale community is intentional. title: Student Union Assembly
text: HomeBuild Leadership SkillsStudent Union Assembly; Our purpose | Governing documents | Weekly SUA meetings | SUA elections; SUA leadership contacts | SUA representatives | Funding requests; SUA committees | Contact an advisor; The Student Union Assembly (SUA) is the official undergraduate student government at UC Santa Cruz, representing over 17,000 students at the campus, local, state, and federal levels. Since its founding in 1985—when it successfully campaigned to make the Banana Slug our official mascot—SUA has been committed to public service and student advocacy.; SUA serves as the official voice of undergraduates on campus-wide and system-wide committees. Through events, rallies, conferences, and forums, SUA promotes civic engagement, activism, and discussion across diverse viewpoints. We also provide training in direct action, student organizing, and representation.;

title: Student Union Assembly
text: The SUA is a proud member of the University of California Student Association (UCSA), connecting UCSC students to systemwide advocacy efforts.; The Student Union Assembly operates under a Constitution and set of Bylaws that guide its structure, responsibilities, and decision-making processes. ; Student Union Assembly Constitution; Student Union Assembly Bylaws; SUA assembly meeting information; Day & time: Tuesdays, 7:30–10:00 p.m.; Location: Winter Quarter: Namaste Lounge + Zoom; Spring Quarter: Oakes Learning Center + Zoom; Zoom Registration: Register here; SUA Assembly meetings are held weekly on Tuesdays and are open to all undergraduate students. Join us in person or via Zoom to stay informed and get involved.; Want to speak at a meeting?; To place an item on the agenda, complete this form or email the SUA Parliamentarian at suaparli@ucsc.edu.

title: Student Union Assembly
text: Please include:; Organization/department (if applicable); A brief description (1–3 sentences) of your topic; The outcome you’re hoping to achieve (e.g., raise awareness, request support, present information); Requests must be submitted at least 72 hours in advance to be considered for the upcoming agenda.; Public records: agenda and minutes; In accordance with the SUA Constitution and the California Ralph M. Brown Act—as adopted through the May 2019 Constitutional Revisions—this page serves as a public record of SUA proceedings and will be updated as follows:; Agendas will be posted at least 72 hours before each regular SUA meeting (typically by Saturday). View Current Agenda; Minutes will be posted within 24 hours after approval by the Assembly (typically by Wednesday). View Approved Minutes;

title: Student Union Assembly
text: For questions, comments, or concerns about SUA transparency or documentation practices, please contact the SUA Parliamentarian at suaparli@ucsc.edu.; SUA Google Drive; The SUA Google Drive is a central hub for important documents and resources. Explore meeting agendas and minutes, committee materials, office and representative folders, adopted resolutions, governing documents, finances, election info, and member resources—all in one place.; The Student Union Assembly (SUA) holds annual elections where undergraduate students vote for their campus-wide representatives. Through this process, students elect SUA Officers and representatives for key leadership roles. Participating in SUA elections is one of the most direct ways students can shape campus policy, funding priorities, and the future of student life at UC Santa Cruz.; To learn more about the election process, including candidate requirements and campaign guidelines, review the SUA Elections Code and the Elections Packet.; SUA officers;

title: Student Union Assembly
text: UMOJA vacant ; Vietnamese Student Association vacant ; Lavender Club vacant ; The Student Union Assembly (SUA) provides funding to support student-led events, programs, and initiatives in service of the undergraduate student body.; Student Organization Funding (SOFA); Registered Student Organizations (RSOs) affiliated with SOAR, Student Media, or OPERS may apply for SUA funding to support events and initiatives that benefit the undergraduate student body. Funding requests must include a detailed budget and be submitted in advance of your event. All applicants are expected to present their proposal at an SUA meeting and follow up with a post-event report. For more information about SOFA funding, contact the SUA Chair at suachair@ucsc.edu.; Sponsorship Requests; Any registered student organization affiliated with a campus department may request sponsorship funding from the SUA.

title: Student Union Assembly
text: To be added to the agenda and present your request, please contact the SUA Parliamentarian at suaparli@ucsc.edu by 5:00 p.m. on the Friday prior to the meeting. SUA will deliberate and vote on your request at the following Assembly meeting.; General programming requests; Per Section E.C.I.d of the SUA Bylaws, any member of the Assembly may request funds from the General Programming line item. Multiple members may also submit a joint request.; Requests for 10% or less of the total line item per member require a simple majority vote by the Assembly.; Requests for more than 10% per member require a two-thirds vote.; To be considered for funding, events or programs must meet the following criteria:; Educational & Enrichment Value: Offers opportunities for personal, social, or educational growth through extracurricular engagement.; Organizational History: Demonstrates progress toward stated goals in past initiatives.;

title: Student Union Assembly
text: Campus Impact: Positively affects the broader campus community and/or includes a service component.; Uniqueness: Avoids duplicating existing programs or services.; Diversity & Accessibility: Supports access and inclusion for historically underrepresented groups.; Student Impact: Clearly outlines the number of students expected to attend, participate, or benefit.; All funding requests must include:; Name, date, time, and location of the event(s); List of sponsors and co-sponsors; Estimated number of student participants or beneficiaries; A clear explanation of the event’s purpose and alignment with the goals of the requesting party, SUA, and the University of California; An itemized budget; For questions or to submit a request, please contact the SUA Parliamentarian at suaparli@ucsc.edu.; The Student Union Assembly (SUA) includes several standing committees that provide opportunities for leadership, advocacy, and campus engagement.

title: Student Union Assembly
text: College-elected representatives are required to serve on at least one logistical committee. Appointed and organization representatives may also join committees if they choose.; Student Organizing Funding Advisory (SOFA) Committee; Chaired by the SUA Treasurer, SOFA reviews and makes recommendations on student organization funding requests. The committee meets twice per quarter. For more information, contact the SUA Treasurer at suatreas@ucsc.edu. ; Lobby Corps; This committee develops and leads advocacy efforts on behalf of UCSC undergraduates. Members engage in local, state, and national lobbying—including visits to the Santa Cruz City Council, the California State Legislature, and Congress in Washington, D.C.; Contact Lobby Corps: ; Legislative Director: sualeg@ucsc.edu ; Lobby Corps Director: sualcd@ucsc.edu; Student Academic Senate; Promotes academic progress by uniting the colleges around shared academic goals and reviewing university policies affecting students.; Diversity Commission;

title: Student Union Assembly
text: Tracks and addresses diversity-related issues across campus, working to improve equity and inclusion at UCSC. For more information, email suavcdi@ucsc.edu.; Student Committee on Committees; Oversees the appointment and tracking of student representatives to campus-wide and administrative committees. For more information, email scocslug@ucsc.edu or apply to join a committee!; Vision Committee; The SUA Vision Committee works to strengthen and improve the Student Union Assembly through research, discussion, and outreach. It fosters collaboration between SUA, student organizations, and student governments, while addressing internal reforms and student concerns. Recent efforts include constitutional revisions and promoting more equitable meeting practices.; Connect with SUA Advisors; Two professional staff members advise and support SUA operations.

title: Student Volunteer Connection
text: HomeVolunteer Opportunities; Volunteers carrying plants; Find your place in service; Whether you’re looking to support your fellow Banana Slugs or connect with the broader Santa Cruz community, there are plenty of ways to make a difference. The UCSC Student Volunteer Center helps students get involved on campus and nearby, while the Volunteer Center of Santa Cruz County offers hundreds of opportunities with local nonprofits and service projects. Explore both to find a cause that speaks to you and start building community through action.; Get involved with the Student Volunteer Center; Looking for meaningful ways to connect, give back, and grow? The UCSC Student Volunteer Center (SVC) is your hub for service—connecting students with local and campus-based volunteer opportunities, useful workshops, and training. Whether you’re passionate about community service or eager to gain leadership and teamwork skills, there’s a spot for you to make a real impact.; Two student volunteering at food drive;

title: Student Volunteer Connection
text: Volunteer in the Santa Cruz Community; Make a difference beyond campus. The Volunteer Center of Santa Cruz County connects students with a wide range of local service opportunities—from food justice and climate action to youth programs and crisis support. Whether you have a few hours or want to commit long-term, you’ll find meaningful ways to get involved and support the broader Santa Cruz community.; Explore volunteer opportunities through the Volunteer Center of Santa Cruz County; Two students volunteering holding wrapped gifts; Last modified: Feb 12, 2026;

title: Summer Session
text: Hit the road from July 1–19 on a 2,400-mile journey across California, from Mendocino to the Salton Sea, the Eastern Sierra, Death Valley, and Big Sur. This immersive field course takes learning beyond the classroom, offering hands-on experience in photographing and exploring the cultural, historical, and environmental landscapes that shape the state.; Slug gaming room with gaming pcs; CMPM 80K; Foundations of Video Game Design; Dive into the world of video games and their power to shape experiences and express ideas. This course invites students to design and interpret games while exploring key topics in the field—no programming experience required. Engage in required synchronous section meetings, with optional events for deeper discussion and collaboration.; Mixing decks; MUSC 58; Songwriting; Craft and Practice; Explore the craft of songwriting through listening, analysis, and hands-on composition.

title: Temporary Staffing Services
text: HomeTalent AcquisitionFilling a VacancyTemporary Staffing Services; Talent Acquisition Staff Directory; Employee Resources for New Appointments; Fair Hiring Guide; Open Recruitment; Campus-Only Recruitment; Non-Recruitment; Waiver of Recruitment; Comparison of Staffing Options; Recruiting and Hiring Resources; I. Summary; Temporary staffing providers may be used by campus units to fill short-term administrative, professional, technical, and light industrial assignments expected to last less than 6 months.; Please note that temporary staffing agency personnel performing cash handling duties are covered by UC Business and Finance Bulletin 49-Policy for Cash and Cash Equivalents Received and therefore required to undergo a background check through the temporary staffing agency. All approved agencies in contract with UC Santa Cruz will ensure their personnel assigned to critical positions have passed a background check before placement begins. ; II. Related Policies, Contract Articles and References; Temporary Staffing Process Summary – Procurement Services; III. Authority;

title: Temporary Staffing Services
text: The Unit Head should consult individuals within their division who have funding authority and/or authority to approve HR actions before initiating a temporary staffing request. Funding approval is based upon divisional delegations of authority.; The Talent Acquisition Office within Staff Human Resources is responsible for monitoring the campuses adherence to length of assignment requirements for temporary staffing agency personnel. Temp staffing assignment extensions require approval of the Talent Acquisition Director.; IV. Criteria; Units are strongly encouraged to fill vacant positions through a Competitive Recruitment; however, temporary staffing services may be used if the assignment has a definite end date that does not exceed 6 months in duration.; V. Process Overview; Division/Unit/Department:; Develop/update the job description, identifying important/critical job functions and duties, and determining the qualifications required for successful performance. Ensure required background checks, DMV record review, and other relevant special conditions of employment are referenced.; Determine the appropriate method for filling the vacancy.

title: Temporary Staffing Services
text: See the Filling a Vacancy guide for a complete list of options. Your Talent Acquisition Consultant can assist with this determination.; Secure funding approval per divisional protocol.; Consult with Procurement Services to determine the best temporary staffing vendor and proceed with the process at the Procurement Services website to acquire temporary staffing services under – How to Initiate a Request for a Temporary Worker.; Temporary Staffing Vendor:; Source candidates, conduct interviews and reference checks and ensure all required background checks are completed prior to the begin date of the assignment. ; Review and approve hours worked using Enterprise Time Capture System (ETC). Access is granted to campus users when the assignment begins.; Monitor completion of the temporary staffing assignment to ensure adherence to campus usage limits. Requests for an extension of assignment must be emailed at least four weeks in advance, to the Talent Acquisition Director, along with a justification for the request.;

title: Temporary Staffing Services
text: Generate paychecks and invoices based upon hours approved by UC Santa Cruz.; Provide monthly ‘length of assignment’ report to Staff Human Resources. Report must be delivered via email no later than the 10th day of the following month.; Staff Human Resources:; Review department email requests to extend temporary staffing assignments and respond in writing.; [Top of Page];

title: Training and Development
text: HomeLearning & Development; AI and Our Work; Upcoming Classes; Leadership Programs; Academic Impressions; Welcome to Learning & Development! Our team is dedicated to addressing the technical and professional development needs of UC Santa Cruz employees.; The objective of Learning and Development is to develop the competencies and to nurture the talents of the UC Santa Cruz workforce. title: UCDC Program
text: HomeProgramsUCDC Washington Program; Capitol Hill; Expand your professional skillset and knowledge by participating in the UCDC (quarter in Washington, D.C.) Program, offering unparalleled access to internships within various industries alongside rigorous coursework in addition to the opportunity to network with professionals and make invaluable connections for future career success.; The UCDC Program supervises and supports students who pursue internships and academic study in the nation’s capital. The program is open to juniors and seniors in all majors. Students enroll for fall, winter, or spring quarter, earning 12-17 course credits, while registered as full-time UCSC students.; Students spend 24-32 hours each week in their internships, from working on Capitol Hill or a government agency to a major media outlet or a nonprofit organization. Internship placements are selected by students based on their interests.;

title: UCDC Program
text: Students also attend a weekly research seminar that enables them to draw on D.C.’s variety of research opportunities to complete a research paper on a subject related to their internships. In addition, students may also take one upper-division elective course from a range of classes offered by faculty at the Washington Center. Elective classes are offered in several disciplines, including the social and natural sciences, arts, and humanities.; For information on resources and upcoming events, follow us on Instagram @ucdc_ucsc; Portrait of a person; “I was an Operations and Community Engagement intern for MetroPrize where I learned about youth development and intervention efforts in the nation’s capital. Today, I work in D.C. and MentorPrize is one of my current job’s partners! My UCDC experience was amazing, humbling, and insightful.”; – Tanya Magaña; Information Sessions;

title: UCDC Program
text: UCDC Information Sessions typically happen on the second Tuesday of every month during the academic year. Registration is required to attend these virtual information sessions. Learn more on the UCDC website.; The UCDC Global Learning Advisor is available by appointment via Zoom or in person.; Quarter & Year Start Date End Date; Fall 2025 September 22, 2025 December 5, 2025; Winter 2026 January 5, 2026 March 13, 2026; Spring 2026 March 23, 2026 June 5, 2026; Students from all majors are encouraged to apply. Admission is competitive. To be eligible for consideration, students must:; Be junior or senior standing (occasionally sophomores); All applicants have to have completed at least two upper-division courses in their major at the time of application; 3.0 GPA or higher; Have a strong academic record; Provide a strong statement of purpose; Provide a strong letter of recommendation (from a UCSC faculty member);

title: UCDC Program
text: Transfer students must complete at least one term at UC Santa Cruz before applying; Final decisions will be made approximately one month from the application deadline. A mandatory orientation for accepted students will happen near the end of the previous quarter prior to departure (e.g., orientation for fall quarter will be held the preceding spring, etc.).; “I interned at the Juvenile Diabetes Research Foundation (JDRF), which allowed me to research health policy, attend meetings with legislators and legislative assistants, and go to briefings on Capitol Hill. I made friends and connections that have led to opportunities that would not have been possible without my internship.”; – Chloe Mietzel; Admission will be based on the student’s statement of purpose, academic record, letter of recommendation, and a personal interview in some cases. Consideration is also given to the distribution of academic disciplines participating in the program. Participation in UCDC changes the way in which a student meets residency requirements for a degree.

title: Undergraduate Education Division (DUE)
text: Advancing access,; creating opportunity; At UC Santa Cruz, the Division of Undergraduate Education brings together a remarkable community dedicated to student success. From the colleges and advising to research and resources that support learning and belonging, we provide the foundation students can depend on from admission through graduation.; 400+; Students employed each year; 17,940; Undergraduate students enrolled; 4,000; Degrees awarded annually; Welcome from the Vice Provost & Dean; Richard Hughey, Vice Provost and Dean of Undergraduate Education; The Division of Undergraduate Education brings together people and programs dedicated to supporting students, from K–12 outreach through graduation. I’m grateful to be part of this collaborative effort and proud of the exceptional leadership team that guides our work.; About Undergraduate Education; Roze Hentschell appointed vice provost and dean of Undergraduate Education;

title: University Advancement
text: Six students sitting on a bench at UC Santa Cruz.; Advancing real change; A dedicated team of talented and committed professionals, UC Santa Cruz University Advancement forms a backbone of support for the campus and its influential work. We communicate about campus achievements and build and nurture relationships with our community—alumni, donors, students, faculty, staff, community members, and others—that inspire engagement with and philanthropic support for the university.; Be a part of the team; At UC Santa Cruz, the promise of tomorrow drives our best work today. University Advancement’s creative and thoughtful people are truly advancing a very special campus—and you could join us. Find out more about open professional opportunities in the division.; Last modified: Jan 12, 2026;

title: Veteran Student Services
text: HomeDiverse Educational PathwaysMilitary-Connected Students Support; Grass and scenic view on UCSC campus; Overview | Programs and services | Incoming and prospective students |; Resources | Connect with our team | Our mission; Quick overview; Who this is for: All military-affiliated students, including:; Veterans; Active duty service members; Reservists and National Guard members; Dependents and spouses of veterans; ROTC cadets; Department of Defense and Homeland Security personnel; What we offer: The Veterans Resource Center fosters community, helps you access your benefits, connects you with on- and off-campus support, and ensures you’re equipped to thrive at UCSC.; Location: Academic Resource Center 121; Hours: Monday–Friday, 9 a.m.–5 p.m.; Email: vets@ucsc.edu ; Phone: (831) 459-5899;

title: Veteran Student Services
text: Instagram: @ucscveterans; Appointments: Schedule via Slug Success; Welcome, military-connected students!; At UC Santa Cruz, we recognize the skills, experiences, and resilience that military-affiliated students bring to our community. The Veterans Resource Center is your space for connection, advocacy, and personalized support—whether you’re navigating the transition to college life, returning to school, or preparing to graduate.; Key programs and services; All veterans receive first-choice enrollment, regardless of benefits status.; Using VA benefits? You’re automatically enrolled.; Not using benefits? Contact the Registrar with proof of service: vets_benefits@ucsc.edu. ; Veteran Benefits Services (via Registrar); Get expert help accessing your VA education benefits. The Veteran Benefits team acts as a liaison with the VA, and can support you with:; Questions about eligibility; Accessing state and federal benefits; DOD Tuition Assistance;

title: Veteran Student Services
text: Monthly Housing Allowance; Email: vets_benefits@ucsc.edu; Need help getting started? Drop-in virtual advising is available with UCSC’s School Certifying Officials. Visit the Veteran Benefits Services website for more information.; Community and events; The Veterans Resource Center hosts events where military-affiliated students can connect, recharge, and build community:; Beach bonfires; Trivia nights & socials; Workshops & fundraisers; Stay updated on Instagram: @ucscveterans ; Steps to apply to UCSC as a veteran; Submit your UC application; Apply for financial aid; Apply for your VA educational benefits; Email your Certificate of Eligibility (COE), DD-214, and verification form to vets_benefits@ucsc.edu; Submit your UCSC Enrollment Verification Form; Questions?

title: Veteran Student Services
text: Call (831) 459-2709 or email vets_benefits@ucsc.edu for assistance.; Additional resources for military-connected students; On-campus partners; Veterans Benefit Services (Registrar’s Office); Off-campus resources; Vets 4 Vets Santa Cruz; Santa Cruz County Veteran Network; VA Supportive Housing (HUD-VASH) Program; Cal Vet; Student Veterans of America ; Senior Living Resources for Veterans ; The staff behind the Veterans Resource Center are deeply committed to supporting military-connected students, including veterans, active duty service members, and their spouses and dependents. Whether you need help transitioning to academic life, accessing military and VA benefits, connecting with the military-connected community, or just want someone to talk to who understands the military experience, we’re here for you. Our approach is student-centered, community-driven, and grounded in the belief that higher education can be transformative.;

title: Veteran Student Services
text: Reach out anytime—we’d love to connect.; The Veterans Resource Center supports all military-affiliated students in reaching their academic and personal goals. We honor your service and are committed to building a campus where you feel supported, connected, and empowered.; Last modified: Dec 04, 2025;

title: Violence in the Workplace Policies and Procedures
text: HomeA-Z Local Process/Resource LocatorViolence in the Workplace; The University of California, Santa Cruz (university) can best achieve its mission of teaching, research, and public service when employees work in an environment that supports the free exchange of ideas and constructive methods of conflict resolution. Intimidation, threats, and acts of violence impede effective communication and undermine employee well-being, the sense of community, and the university’s ability to achieve its mission. Moreover, these destructive behaviors erode the rights of employees to a violence-free Workplace under California law and university policy.; Where to get help, Behavioral Intervention Team (BIT) contact information, and the full policy text can be found at; Workplace Violence. The purpose of this policy is to codify the university’s commitment to a violence-free workplace.; Last modified: Aug 07, 2025;

title: Visiting UC Santa Cruz
text: The UC Santa Cruz campus takes the breath away while expanding the mind. Come visit us. You’ll see.; Where we are; UC Santa Cruz, located just above Monterey Bay on California’s Central Coast, is uniquely positioned at the intersection of technology and agriculture. Just 45 minutes from the high-tech center of Silicon Valley and 25 minutes from the fertile farmlands of Watsonville, our geographic placement has helped us practice our social justice values as we seek to develop technology for the social good. ; In addition, the Santa Cruz area’s diverse community and vibrant cultural scene mean students live among towering redwoods and misty ocean fog while also being close to city happenings, social engagement, cultural events, and opportunities for learning, growth, and recreation. ; One campus, many sites; In addition to the 2,000-acre main residential campus, UCSC sites include: ; Monterey Bay Education, Science and Technology (MBEST) Research Park; Closest airports;

title: Visiting UC Santa Cruz
text: UCSC is about 75 miles south of San Francisco. The closest airport is San Jose Mineta International Airport. San Francisco International Airport is also an option. Highways 1 and 17 serve Santa Cruz.; Get a feel for the lay of the land before you step foot on campus. ; Transportation and parking; Walking is often the best way to experience campus. Shuttles and buses provide convenient transportation across campus and between the colleges and academic centers.; Find all the information you need about parking and getting around.; Campus destinations; We welcome your visit to UC Santa Cruz! Many places and programs on campus are open to the public to tour, attend, and enjoy. Make sure to include some of these iconic campus spots on your visit for the full UCSC experience.; Plan your visit; While you’re here; Find an event or experience some of the creative and intellectual energy on campus. Explore the options and choose your adventure.;

Take a tour—in person or virtually—and see for yourself. ; Take a campus tour; Last modified: Jan 09, 2026;

title: Women’s Center
text: The Women's Center; Someone at the women's center working on laptop in front of library book case; Affirming the dignity and diversity of all women; Est. 1985; The UCSC Women’s Center affirms the dignity and diversity of all women. The center upholds and challenges feminist traditions by creating a community space for all women and their allies to achieve individual and social change.; Resource guide; The Women’s Center offers a variety of resources for all. Our comprehensive online resource guide connects you with UCSC Resource Centers and additional support networks. Visit our resource page to explore both on-campus and off-campus services and support that best fit your needs.; Visit our resource guide; Oakes bridge; The Women’s Center hosts several events throughout the year. We have annual events such as our Welcome Fall Ball, Makers’ Markets, Women’s Award Gala, and Shatter the Stigma.

title: Women’s Center
text: We offer a wide range of programs– from creative activities like collaging and art, to supportive spaces for discussion, connection, and more.; Two people from the women's center team; Check out the Women’s Center Instagram; Check out the Women’s Center TikTok; The Women’s Center is part of a network of campus resource centers that work together to support all students. In collaboration with centers like AARCC, AIRC, El Centro, AA/PIRC, and the Cantú Queer Center, we offer shared programs, resources, and spaces that celebrate identity, build community, and promote belonging. In addition to exploring our offerings, we encourage you to check out the many events, services, and opportunities provided by our partner centers across campus. UCSC resource centers are open to all students, and everyone is welcome.;

title: Women’s Club (Friends Group)
text: Community Connections; Where campus and community come together; Members of university friends' groups from UC Berkeley, UC Davis, UC Merced, and UC Santa Cruz gather at the UCSC Arboretum for a tour during InterCampus Gathering Day in March 2025.; The purpose of UCSC Community Connections is to share common interests, to promote understanding and support for the purpose and functions of the University, and to raise funds for and award scholarships to re-entry students and local students pursuing degrees at UCSC.; (Left) Members of university friends’ groups from UC Berkeley, UC Davis, UC Merced, and UC Santa Cruz gather at the UCSC Arboretum for a tour during InterCampus Gathering Day in March 2025.; 2026 Events; July 1, 2026 – Memberships Renewable for the fiscal year.; October 7, 2026, 11 a.m.-1 p.m. – Fall Gathering with music by Fiat Musica.

title: Writing Program
text: Decorative cover banner image of the exterior of the Humanities building.; Making Writing Happen at UCSC; The Writing Program provides writing instruction to all incoming frosh. We offer some of the smallest classes on campus, and we’re committed teachers who are attentive to student work, innovative in the classroom, and devoted to preparing students for writing at the university and in daily life. We work to support writing as an integral part of the student experience at UCSC.; Writing Electives; Fall 2026 First Years: Go Here!; Writing Center logo with its tagline: Write On!; Looking for writing assistance?; Check out our home page:; The Writing Center @UCSC; Faculty: Want our Writing Assistants to visit your class to introduce the Writing Center?; UCSC McHenry library, lit up at dusk.
